question: What did we conclude was causing WebSocket connections to drop exactly 60 seconds after becoming idle?
turns: WebSocket connections to our real-time notification service drop exactly 60 seconds after the last message is exchanged. Clients auto-reconnect but there's a visible gap in updates. Happens consistently regardless of client platform.
Checked the WebSocket server (Node.js ws library v8.14): no server-side idle timeout is configured. The ws server is set to keep connections indefinitely. Nothing in the app closes them.

Looked at the nginx config for the /ws location block. There's no proxy_read_timeout directive set there, so it inherits the nginx default of 60 seconds. After 60s with no data from the upstream ws server, nginx closes the TCP connection unilaterally.

Added proxy_read_timeout 3600s and proxy_send_timeout 3600s to the /ws location block. Connections now stay alive for hours. Root cause: nginx proxy_read_timeout defaulting to 60 seconds, silently closing idle WebSocket connections.
